title: Add helm-validate CI job with lint and template tests
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#121](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/121)

Add a `helm-validate` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` gated by a new `helm` path filter (`operators/keystone/helm/**`) in the existing `dorny/paths-filter` config. The job: (1) sets up Helm via `azure/setup-helm`, (2) installs helm-unittest plugin with pinned version via `helm plugin install`, (3) runs `helm lint` on the keystone-operator chart, (4) runs `helm template` with 4 value override scenarios (defaults, webhook.enabled=false, serviceAccount.create=false+serviceAccount.name=existing-sa, minimal resource overrides), (5) runs `helm unittest`. Create unittest files under `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/` for deployment (image, replicas, resources, securityContext, probes, leader-elect arg, webhook volume mount), clusterrole (RBAC rules coverage), clusterrolebinding (role ref + subject), service (metrics port, conditional webhook port), and webhook-configuration (present when enabled, absent when disabled).

**Rationale:** The CI pipeline validates Go code but has zero Helm chart validation. Template errors, broken value wiring, and invalid YAML are only caught at deploy time. This has caused silent breakage when chart values were renamed or conditionals were misconfigured. Adding lint+template+unittest catches these issues at PR time and prevents broken releases.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/ci.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/deployment_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrole_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrolebinding_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/service_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/webhook_test.yaml

stories.title: CI Maintainer validates Helm chart at PR time to catch template errors before merge
stories.want: a helm-validate CI job that runs helm lint, helm template, and helm-unittest on every PR touching Helm chart files
stories.so_that: template errors, broken value wiring, and invalid YAML are caught at PR time instead of at deploy time
criteria: helm-validate job appears in CI workflow and runs on PRs that change files under operators/keystone/helm/**
Job exits non-zero when helm lint detects chart structure or syntax errors
Job exits non-zero when helm template fails to render for any of the 4 value override scenarios
Job exits non-zero when helm-unittest reports test failures

stories.title: CI Maintainer avoids unnecessary CI cost by gating helm-validate on path filter
stories.want: the helm-validate job to run only when Helm chart files change, not on every PR
stories.so_that: CI resources are not wasted validating unchanged charts (e.g. docs-only or Go-only changes)
criteria: A new 'helm' path filter exists in the changes job matching operators/keystone/helm/**
helm-validate job has condition: needs.changes.outputs.helm == 'true'
helm filter is forced true on tag pushes (v*) to validate before chart publish
helm-validate does not run when only Go, docs, or E2E infrastructure files change

stories.title: CI Maintainer catches broken value wiring via template rendering scenarios
stories.want: helm template to render the chart with 4 value override scenarios covering all conditional branches
stories.so_that: renamed values, broken conditionals, and invalid YAML in conditional blocks are detected
criteria: Scenario 1: helm template with default values renders without error
Scenario 2: helm template with webhook.enabled=false renders without error (no webhook resources)
Scenario 3: helm template with serviceAccount.create=false and serviceAccount.name=existing-sa renders without error
Scenario 4: helm template with resources.limits.cpu=100m and resources.limits.memory=64Mi renders without error

stories.title: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
stories.want: helm-unittest tests for the deployment template covering image, replicas, resources, securityContext, probes, args, and webhook volume mount
stories.so_that: changes to the deployment template that break security hardening, health checks, or conditional logic are caught automatically
criteria: deployment_test.yaml asserts correct container image using values.image.repository and Chart.AppVersion
deployment_test.yaml asserts replicas=2, resource limits/requests match defaults
deployment_test.yaml asserts pod and container securityContext (runAsNonRoot, user 65532, no privilege escalation)
deployment_test.yaml asserts probes (/healthz, /readyz on port 8081), args (--leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address), and conditional webhook volume mount

stories.title: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
stories.want: helm-unittest tests for clusterrole, clusterrolebinding, service, and webhook-configuration templates
stories.so_that: accidental removal of RBAC rules, broken port configuration, and webhook conditional rendering errors are caught
criteria: clusterrole_test.yaml asserts all 12 RBAC rule blocks covering keystones, deployments, services, jobs, mariadb, externalsecrets, networkpolicies, HPAs, PDBs, and leases
clusterrolebinding_test.yaml asserts roleRef and ServiceAccount subject
service_test.yaml asserts metrics port (8080) and conditional webhook port (443->9443)
webhook_test.yaml asserts MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration render when enabled and are absent when disabled

requirements.description: The changes job SHALL include a 'helm' path filter matching 'operators/keystone/helm/**' and expose it as a job output
requirements.rationale: Gates the helm-validate job to run only when Helm chart files change, following the existing dorny/paths-filter pattern

scenarios.name: Helm filter triggers on chart change
scenarios.when: a PR modifies any file under operators/keystone/helm/
scenarios.then: the changes job outputs helm=true
and_then: helm-validate job condition is satisfied and the job runs

scenarios.name: Helm filter does not trigger on Go-only change
scenarios.when: a PR modifies only Go source files under operators/keystone/internal/
scenarios.then: the changes job outputs helm=false
and_then: helm-validate job is skipped

scenarios.name: Helm filter forced true on tag push
scenarios.when: a v* tag is pushed
scenarios.then: the changes job outputs helm=true regardless of which files changed
and_then: helm-validate runs on every release to validate the chart before publishing

requirements.description: The helm-validate job SHALL set up Helm via azure/setup-helm and install helm-unittest plugin with a pinned version
requirements.rationale: Reproducible CI requires pinned tool versions; azure/setup-helm is already used by helm-push and github-release jobs

scenarios.name: Helm CLI available after setup
scenarios.when: the azure/setup-helm step completes
scenarios.then: the helm binary is available on PATH
and_then: subsequent steps can invoke helm lint, helm template, and helm plugin commands

scenarios.name: helm-unittest plugin installed with pinned version
scenarios.when: the plugin install step runs
scenarios.then: helm unittest command is available with the specified version
and_then: the version is deterministic across CI runs

scenarios.name: azure/setup-helm is SHA-pinned following project convention
scenarios.when: the action reference is inspected
scenarios.then: it uses the same SHA hash as existing helm-push job (dda3372f752e03dde6b3237bc9431cdc2f7a02a2 # v5)

requirements.description: The helm-validate job SHALL run helm lint on the keystone-operator chart and fail on errors
requirements.rationale: helm lint validates chart structure, Chart.yaml metadata, template syntax, and value type correctness

scenarios.name: Lint passes on valid chart
scenarios.when: helm lint is run against operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0
and_then: the CI step succeeds and subsequent steps execute

scenarios.name: Lint fails on invalid template syntax
scenarios.when: a template file contains invalid Go template syntax (e.g., unclosed {{ }})
scenarios.then: helm lint exits with non-zero code
and_then: the CI job fails and blocks merge

scenarios.name: Lint fails on invalid Chart.yaml
scenarios.when: Chart.yaml is missing required fields or has invalid values
scenarios.then: helm lint exits with non-zero code
and_then: the CI job fails and blocks merge

requirements.description: The helm-validate job SHALL run helm template with 4 value override scenarios and fail on render errors
requirements.rationale: Template rendering with different value combinations exercises all conditional branches (webhook, serviceAccount, resources) and catches broken wiring

scenarios.name: Scenario 1: default values render successfully
scenarios.when: helm template is run with no value overrides
scenarios.then: all templates render without error
and_then: output includes Deployment, Service, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, ServiceAccount, webhook configs

scenarios.name: Scenario 2: webhook disabled renders successfully
scenarios.when: helm template is run with --set webhook.enabled=false
scenarios.then: templates render without error
and_then: output excludes MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, Certificate, Issuer, and webhook port/volume

scenarios.name: Scenario 3: existing service account renders successfully
scenarios.when: helm template is run with --set serviceAccount.create=false --set serviceAccount.name=existing-sa
scenarios.then: templates render without error
and_then: output excludes ServiceAccount resource and deployment references 'existing-sa'

scenarios.name: Scenario 4: minimal resource overrides render successfully
scenarios.when: helm template is run with --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=64Mi
scenarios.then: templates render without error
and_then: resource values in rendered output reflect the overrides

requirements.description: The helm-validate job SHALL run helm unittest against test files in the chart's tests/ directory and fail on test failures
requirements.rationale: helm-unittest provides declarative assertion testing for Helm templates, catching value wiring and conditional rendering errors

scenarios.name: All unit tests pass
scenarios.when: helm unittest is run against operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0
and_then: all test suites report success

scenarios.name: Test failure blocks CI
scenarios.when: a unit test assertion fails (e.g., wrong port number)
scenarios.then: helm unittest exits with non-zero code
and_then: the CI job fails and blocks merge

scenarios.name: Tests discovered from tests/ directory
scenarios.when: helm unittest is invoked on the chart directory
scenarios.then: it discovers and runs all *_test.yaml files under tests/
and_then: deployment_test.yaml, clusterrole_test.yaml, clusterrolebinding_test.yaml, service_test.yaml, webhook_test.yaml are all executed

requirements.description: deployment_test.yaml SHALL assert container image, replicas, resources, securityContext, probes, leader-elect arg, and conditional webhook volume mount
requirements.rationale: The deployment template is the most complex template with multiple conditional branches and security-critical settings

scenarios.name: Default values produce correct deployment
scenarios.when: the deployment template is rendered with default values
scenarios.then: replicas=2, image=ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator:0.1.0, resources match defaults
and_then: securityContext sets runAsNonRoot=true, runAsUser=65532, container drops ALL capabilities

scenarios.name: Probes configured correctly
scenarios.when: the deployment template is rendered
scenarios.then: livenessProbe.httpGet.path=/healthz on port 8081, readinessProbe.httpGet.path=/readyz on port 8081

scenarios.name: Leader election arg present by default
scenarios.when: the deployment template is rendered with default values (leaderElection.enabled=true)
scenarios.then: container args include --leader-elect
and_then: args also include --metrics-bind-address=:8080 and --health-probe-bind-address=:8081

scenarios.name: Webhook volume mount present when enabled
scenarios.when: the deployment template is rendered with webhook.enabled=true (default)
scenarios.then: container has volumeMount for webhook-certs at /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs (readOnly)
and_then: pod has volume referencing the webhook-cert secret

scenarios.name: Webhook volume mount absent when disabled
scenarios.when: the deployment template is rendered with webhook.enabled=false
scenarios.then: container has no volumeMounts
and_then: pod has no volumes section
webhook container port (9443) is absent

requirements.description: clusterrole_test.yaml SHALL assert all 12 RBAC rule blocks covering all managed API groups and resources
requirements.rationale: Accidental removal or modification of RBAC rules causes operator failures at runtime; static assertion catches drift

scenarios.name: All expected API groups have rules
scenarios.when: the clusterrole template is rendered
scenarios.then: rules cover keystone.openstack.c5c3.io, apps, core (""), batch, k8s.mariadb.com, external-secrets.io, rbac.authorization.k8s.io, policy, autoscaling, networking.k8s.io, coordination.k8s.io

scenarios.name: Verb restrictions are correct
scenarios.when: the clusterrole template is rendered
scenarios.then: events rule has only create and patch verbs, externalsecrets rule has no delete verb
and_then: all other resource rules include get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete

scenarios.name: Specific resources are present per API group
scenarios.when: the clusterrole template is rendered
scenarios.then: core group includes services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts; batch includes jobs and cronjobs
and_then: policy includes poddisruptionbudgets, autoscaling includes horizontalpodautoscalers, networking.k8s.io includes networkpolicies

requirements.description: clusterrolebinding_test.yaml SHALL assert the roleRef references the correct ClusterRole and the subject references the correct ServiceAccount
requirements.rationale: A broken binding silently prevents the operator from having any RBAC permissions

scenarios.name: RoleRef points to the matching ClusterRole
scenarios.when: the clusterrolebinding template is rendered
scenarios.then: roleRef.kind=ClusterRole, roleRef.apiGroup=rbac.authorization.k8s.io, roleRef.name matches the fullname

scenarios.name: Subject is the operator ServiceAccount
scenarios.when: the clusterrolebinding template is rendered
scenarios.then: subjects[0].kind=ServiceAccount, subjects[0].name matches serviceAccountName, subjects[0].namespace matches Release.Namespace

requirements.description: service_test.yaml SHALL assert metrics port configuration and conditional webhook port
requirements.rationale: Incorrect port configuration breaks metrics scraping and webhook admission callbacks

scenarios.name: Metrics port matches values
scenarios.when: the service template is rendered with default values
scenarios.then: ports include metrics with port=8080, targetPort=8080
and_then: service type is ClusterIP

scenarios.name: Webhook port present when enabled
scenarios.when: the service template is rendered with webhook.enabled=true (default)
scenarios.then: ports include webhook with port=443, targetPort=9443

scenarios.name: Webhook port absent when disabled
scenarios.when: the service template is rendered with webhook.enabled=false
scenarios.then: ports do not include a webhook entry
and_then: only the metrics port is present

requirements.description: webhook_test.yaml SHALL assert webhook configurations render when enabled and are absent when disabled
requirements.rationale: Webhook resources left behind when disabled cause API server errors; missing resources when enabled breaks admission

scenarios.name: Both webhook configs render when enabled
scenarios.when: the webhook-configuration template is rendered with webhook.enabled=true
scenarios.then: output contains MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
and_then: MutatingWebhookConfiguration has name ending in -mutating, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration has name ending in -validating

scenarios.name: No webhook resources when disabled
scenarios.when: the webhook-configuration template is rendered with webhook.enabled=false
scenarios.then: the template produces no output (empty manifest)

scenarios.name: cert-manager annotation is correct
scenarios.when: the webhook-configuration template is rendered with webhook.enabled=true
scenarios.then: both webhook configs have annotation cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from referencing the correct Certificate name

requirements.description: The CI workflow reference documentation SHALL be updated to include the helm-validate job
requirements.rationale: docs/reference/ci-workflow.md documents all CI jobs; omitting helm-validate creates documentation drift

scenarios.name: Job description added to docs
scenarios.when: docs/reference/ci-workflow.md is read
scenarios.then: a helm-validate section exists describing steps (setup, lint, template scenarios, unittest)
and_then: the section follows the same format as existing job descriptions (table of steps)

scenarios.name: Path filter documented
scenarios.when: the Path Filters section of the documentation is read
scenarios.then: the helm filter is listed with its matching pattern operators/keystone/helm/**
and_then: the Job Dependency DAG section shows helm-validate as an independent gate job

tasks.title: Add helm path filter and helm-validate job to CI workflow (CC-0041, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Modify .github/workflows/ci.yaml to: (1) Add 'helm' filter in dorny/paths-filter matching 'operators/keystone/helm/**'. (2) Add 'helm' output to the changes job outputs: helm: ${{ steps.result.outputs.helm }}. (3) In the 'Resolve effective changes' step, add 'echo "helm=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"' in the tag-push block and 'echo "helm=${{ steps.filter.outputs.helm }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"' in the non-tag else block. (4) Add a new 'helm-validate' job after the docs job with: needs: [changes], if: needs.changes.outputs.helm == 'true', runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 10, steps: checkout (SHA-pinned), azure/setup-helm@dda3372f752e03dde6b3237bc9431cdc2f7a02a2 # v5, install helm-unittest plugin (pinned version), helm lint, helm template (4 scenarios: defaults, webhook.enabled=false, serviceAccount.create=false+serviceAccount.name=existing-sa, resources.limits.cpu=100m+resources.limits.memory=64Mi), helm unittest.

tasks.title: Create deployment_test.yaml helm-unittest file (CC-0041, REQ-006)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/deployment_test.yaml with helm-unittest assertions for: container image (repository + appVersion tag), replicas (2), resources (limits and requests), pod securityContext (runAsNonRoot, user 65532, group 65532, fsGroup, seccomp RuntimeDefault), container securityContext (no privilege escalation, drop ALL, readOnlyRootFilesystem, seccomp), liveness probe (/healthz:8081), readiness probe (/readyz:8081), container args (--leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address=:8080, --health-probe-bind-address=:8081), container ports (metrics 8080, health 8081, webhook 9443 conditional), webhook volume mount (present when enabled, absent when disabled), webhook volume (present when enabled, absent when disabled). Use 'set' to test webhook.enabled=false scenario.

tasks.title: Create clusterrole_test.yaml helm-unittest file (CC-0041, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrole_test.yaml with helm-unittest assertions covering all 12 RBAC rule blocks in templates/clusterrole.yaml. Assert each apiGroup and its resources are present: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io (keystones, keystones/status, keystones/finalizers), apps (deployments), core/"" (services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts + events), batch (jobs, cronjobs), k8s.mariadb.com (databases, users, grants), external-secrets.io (externalsecrets, pushsecrets), rbac.authorization.k8s.io (roles, rolebindings), policy (poddisruptionbudgets), autoscaling (horizontalpodautoscalers), networking.k8s.io (networkpolicies), coordination.k8s.io (leases). Verify verb restrictions: events has only [create, patch], externalsecrets has no delete.

tasks.title: Create clusterrolebinding_test.yaml helm-unittest file (CC-0041, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrolebinding_test.yaml with helm-unittest assertions for: kind is ClusterRoleBinding, roleRef.apiGroup=rbac.authorization.k8s.io, roleRef.kind=ClusterRole, roleRef.name matches fullname, subjects[0].kind=ServiceAccount, subjects[0].name matches serviceAccountName, subjects[0].namespace matches Release.Namespace.

tasks.title: Create service_test.yaml helm-unittest file (CC-0041, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/service_test.yaml with helm-unittest assertions for: service type=ClusterIP, metrics port (name=metrics, port=8080, targetPort=8080, protocol=TCP), conditional webhook port with webhook.enabled=true (name=webhook, port=443, targetPort=9443, protocol=TCP), and webhook port absent with webhook.enabled=false. Use 'set' to test both webhook enabled and disabled scenarios.

tasks.title: Create webhook_test.yaml helm-unittest file (CC-0041, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/webhook_test.yaml with helm-unittest assertions for: MutatingWebhookConfiguration rendered when webhook.enabled=true (name ending -mutating, webhook path, admissionReviewVersions, failurePolicy, sideEffects, rules), ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rendered when webhook.enabled=true (name ending -validating, webhook path, operations include DELETE), cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation on both configs, and no documents rendered when webhook.enabled=false. Use documentIndex to select individual documents from the multi-document template.

tasks.title: Update CI workflow reference documentation for helm-validate job (CC-0041, REQ-011)
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/ci-workflow.md to document the new helm-validate job. Add: (1) 'helm' entry to the Path Filters section with pattern operators/keystone/helm/**. (2) helm-validate to the Job Dependency DAG (independent gate job, like docs). (3) A new 'helm-validate' subsection under Jobs following the existing format (table of steps, timeout, description of each step). (4) 'helm: ${{ steps.result.outputs.helm }}' to the changes job outputs table. Follow the existing documentation style (markdown tables, step descriptions, CC feature ID references).

test_specifications.test_function: should render with default image and replicas
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Deployment has replicas=2, container image=ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator:0.1.0, imagePullPolicy=IfNotPresent [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should set resource limits and requests from values
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Container resources: limits.cpu=500m, limits.memory=128Mi, requests.cpu=10m, requests.memory=64Mi [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should configure pod security context with non-root user 65532
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Pod securityContext: runAsNonRoot=true, runAsUser=65532, runAsGroup=65532, fsGroup=65532, seccompProfile.type=RuntimeDefault [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should configure container security context with no privilege escalation
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Container securityContext: allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, capabilities.drop=[ALL], readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, seccompProfile.type=RuntimeDefault [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should configure liveness and readiness probes on port 8081
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: livenessProbe.httpGet: path=/healthz port=8081; readinessProbe.httpGet: path=/readyz port=8081 [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include leader-elect arg when leader election enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Container args contain --leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address=:8080, --health-probe-bind-address=:8081 [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include webhook volume mount when webhook enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Container has volumeMount name=webhook-certs, mountPath=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs, readOnly=true; pod has matching volume [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should not include webhook volume mount when webhook disabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=false: container has no volumeMounts, pod has no volumes, no webhook port (9443) [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include webhook container port when webhook enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches deployment template regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Container ports include metrics (8080), health (8081), and webhook (9443) when webhook.enabled=true [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include all expected RBAC rules
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Rules cover all 12 API groups: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io (keystones, status, finalizers), apps (deployments), core (services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts, events), batch (jobs, cronjobs), k8s.mariadb.com (databases, users, grants), external-secrets.io, rbac.authorization.k8s.io, policy, autoscaling, networking.k8s.io, coordination.k8s.io [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should restrict verbs correctly for events and externalsecrets
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Events rule has only [create, patch] verbs; externalsecrets rule has [get, list, watch, create, update, patch] without delete [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should reference the correct ClusterRole
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: roleRef: apiGroup=rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind=ClusterRole, name=RELEASE-NAME-keystone-operator [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should bind to the operator ServiceAccount in release namespace
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: subjects[0]: kind=ServiceAccount, name=RELEASE-NAME-keystone-operator, namespace=NAMESPACE [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should expose metrics port from values
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Service type=ClusterIP, ports include name=metrics port=8080 targetPort=8080 protocol=TCP [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include webhook port when webhook enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=true: ports include name=webhook port=443 targetPort=9443 protocol=TCP [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should not include webhook port when webhook disabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=false: ports do not contain name=webhook; only metrics port present [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render MutatingWebhookConfiguration when webhook enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=true: MutatingWebhookConfiguration rendered with name ending -mutating, webhook path /mutate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render ValidatingWebhookConfiguration when webhook enabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=true: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rendered with name ending -validating, webhook path /validate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should not render webhook resources when webhook disabled
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: With webhook.enabled=false: template produces no documents (hasDocuments count 0) [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should include cert-manager inject-ca-from annotation
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches RBAC, service, and webhook regressions via unit tests
test_specifications.expected: Both webhook configs have annotation cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from: NAMESPACE/RELEASE-NAME-keystone-operator-webhook [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: [manual-verification] helm lint passes on current chart
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer validates Helm chart at PR time to catch template errors before merge
test_specifications.expected: Running 'helm lint operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/' locally exits with code 0 [integration]

test_specifications.test_function: [manual-verification] helm template default scenario renders
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches broken value wiring via template rendering scenarios
test_specifications.expected: Running 'helm template test operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/' locally produces valid YAML with all expected resources [integration]

test_specifications.test_function: [manual-verification] helm template webhook-disabled scenario renders
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer catches broken value wiring via template rendering scenarios
test_specifications.expected: Running 'helm template test operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/ --set webhook.enabled=false' produces valid YAML excluding webhook resources [integration]

test_specifications.test_function: [manual-verification] helm unittest passes on current chart
test_specifications.story: CI Maintainer validates Helm chart at PR time to catch template errors before merge
test_specifications.expected: Running 'helm unittest operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/' locally exits with code 0 and all test suites pass [integration]

review_criteria: All CI YAML changes are syntactically valid (yamllint or equivalent passes)
azure/setup-helm action uses the same SHA-pinned reference as existing helm-push job (dda3372f752e03dde6b3237bc9431cdc2f7a02a2 # v5)
helm-unittest plugin version is pinned to a specific version, not @latest or a mutable tag
All 5 test files (deployment, clusterrole, clusterrolebinding, service, webhook) pass when running 'helm unittest operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/' locally
helm lint exits 0 on the current chart: 'helm lint operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/'
All 4 helm template scenarios exit 0 without rendering errors
helm path filter output is correctly wired: added to dorny/paths-filter config, exposed as job output, forced true on tag pushes, forwarded in non-tag else block
docs/reference/ci-workflow.md is updated with helm-validate job documentation following existing format
No existing CI jobs are modified (no dependency changes to e2e-operator, helm-push, etc.)
helm-validate job follows existing CI conventions: SHA-pinned actions with version comments, timeout set, needs/if conditions
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This feature adds a single new CI job (`helm-validate`) to the existing GitHub Actions workflow and creates 5 helm-unittest test files. No existing jobs are modified.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ .github/workflows/ci.yaml                            │
│                                                      │
│  changes job                                         │
│  ├─ dorny/paths-filter (+ helm filter)               │
│  └─ Resolve effective changes (+ helm output)        │
│                                                      │
│  helm-validate job  [NEW]                            │
│  ├─ actions/checkout                                 │
│  ├─ azure/setup-helm                                 │
│  ├─ helm plugin install (helm-unittest, pinned)      │
│  ├─ helm lint                                        │
│  ├─ helm template (4 scenarios)                      │
│  └─ helm unittest                                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │
               reads test files from
                         ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/      │
│  ├─ deployment_test.yaml                             │
│  ├─ clusterrole_test.yaml                            │
│  ├─ clusterrolebinding_test.yaml                     │
│  ├─ service_test.yaml                                │
│  └─ webhook_test.yaml                                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data Flow

1. PR is opened → GitHub triggers CI workflow
2. `changes` job runs dorny/paths-filter → determines `helm=true/false`
3. If `helm=true`: `helm-validate` job starts
4. Job sets up Helm CLI + installs helm-unittest plugin
5. `helm lint` validates chart structure and syntax
6. `helm template` renders chart with 4 value combinations → validates rendering
7. `helm unittest` runs test files from `tests/` → validates template output assertions
8. Job result blocks merge via branch protection if any step fails

## Key Patterns to Follow

- **Action pinning**: Use full SHA hash with `# v<version>` comment (e.g., `actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6`)
- **Path filter**: Follow existing `dorny/paths-filter` pattern; add `helm` alongside `go_common`, `keystone`, `docs`, `e2e_infra`
- **Output resolution**: Mirror the docs/e2e-infra pattern in the 'Resolve effective changes' step; force `helm=true` on tag pushes
- **Job structure**: Match existing job layout — `needs`, `if`, `runs-on`, `timeout-minutes`, then steps
- **helm-unittest syntax**: Use `suite`, `templates`, `tests` top-level keys; `it`, `set`, `asserts` per test case; `documentIndex` for multi-document templates

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **helm-unittest plugin install**: The `helm plugin install` command clones from GitHub; network issues could cause flaky CI. Consider caching the plugin in the future (deferred).
2. **Multi-document templates**: webhook-configuration.yaml renders 2 documents (MutatingWebhookConfiguration + ValidatingWebhookConfiguration). Use `documentIndex: 0` and `documentIndex: 1` in test assertions to target each document.
3. **Template helper evaluation**: helm-unittest evaluates template helpers, so fullname will resolve to `RELEASE-NAME-keystone-operator` in test assertions (default release name is RELEASE-NAME).
4. **helm template --set with dots**: Values like `resources.limits.cpu` use dots as path separators in `--set` flags. This is standard Helm behavior and works correctly.
5. **Empty template output**: When `webhook.enabled=false`, the webhook-configuration.yaml template produces no output. Use `hasDocuments` with `count: 0` to assert this.
